zh.lifeSummary: 當Humphrey VI de Bohun於1248年12月31日出生在英國艾可時，他的父親Humphrey de Bohun V25歲，母親Eleanor de Braose20歲。他和Mathilde de Fiennes至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。他於1275年被列為constable of england。他去世於1298年12月31日在英國艾可普萊享年50歲，埋葬在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登瓦爾登 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bohun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Moon
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humphry
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

nameMeanings.name: VI
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Humphrey VI de Bohun was born on 31 December 1248, in Essex, England, his father, Humphrey de Bohun V, was 25 and his mother, Eleanor de Braose, was 20. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Mathilde de Fiennes. In 1275, his occupation is listed as constable of england. He died on 31 December 1298, in Pleshey, Essex, England, at the age of 50, and was buried in Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Humphrey VI de Bohun est né le 31 décembre 1248, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, Humphrey de Bohun V, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Braose, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Mathilde de Fiennes. En 1275, sa profession de son indiquée est constable of england. Il est décédé le 31 décembre 1298, à Pleshey, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre.

de.Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, Eng: Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, England
de.lifeSummary: Als Humphrey VI de Bohun am 31. Dezember 1248 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Humphrey de Bohun V 25 und seine Mutter Eleanor de Braose 20. Er hatte mit Mathilde de Fiennes mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1275 ist sein Beruf als constable of england aufgeführt. Er starb am 31. Dezember 1298 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Pleshey, Essex, England und wurde in Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Humphrey VI de Bohun è nato il il 31 dicembre 1248 nel Essex, England, il suo padre, Humphrey de Bohun V, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor de Braose, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Mathilde de Fiennes. Nel 1275, la sua occupazione è indicata come constable of england. Morì il 31 dicembre 1298, nel Pleshey, Essex, England, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Humphrey VI de Bohunが1248年12月31日にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父Humphrey de Bohun Vは25歳，母Eleanor de Braoseは20歳であった。彼はMathilde de Fiennesとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。1275年，職業はconstable of englandと記録されている。彼は1298年12月31日にイギリスエセックスプレシーで50歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンワルデン アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, Eng: 영국 에세스 사프런 월든 월든 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey VI de Bohun(이)가 1248년 12월 31일 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Humphrey de Bohun V(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor de Braose(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Mathilde de Fiennes(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1275년, 그의 직업은 constable of england이었다. 그는 1298년 12월 31일 영국 에세스 플레셰이에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 사프런 월든 월든 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Humphrey VI de Bohun nasceu em 31 de dezembro de 1248, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Humphrey de Bohun V, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor de Braose, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas com Mathilde de Fiennes. Em 1275, sua profissão aparece como constable of england. Ele faleceu em 31 de dezembro de 1298, em Pleshey, Essex, Inglaterra, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.Walden Abbey, Saffron Walden, Essex, Eng: валден эбби, саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Humphrey VI de Bohun родился 31 декабря 1248 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Humphrey de Bohun V было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Braose, 20. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Mathilde de Fiennes. В 1275 году он работал по профессии constable of england. Он умер 31 декабря 1298 г. в местности плеши, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности валден эбби, саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия.
